title: Whole Milk for Healthy Kids Act of 2025
short_title: Whole Milk for Healthy Kids Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill changes the rules about what kinds of milk schools can serve in their lunch programs. Schools will now be able to offer whole milk, reduced-fat milk, low-fat milk, and fat-free milk to students, and all of these can be flavored or unflavored and organic or regular. The bill also says that when schools count saturated fat in meals, they don't have to count the fat that comes from milk. Schools are not allowed to buy milk from companies owned by the Chinese government, and parents can now write notes asking for milk substitutes for their children instead of only doctors being able to do this.
last_action_text: Placed on the Union Calendar, Calendar No. 111.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Whole Milk for Healthy Kids Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2  Organic or Non-Organic Whole Milk Permissible
sections.text: This section changes the rules about what kinds of milk schools can serve in their lunch programs. Schools that take part in the national school lunch program must offer students different types of liquid milk. They can now serve students both flavored and plain milk that is either organic or regular. This includes whole milk, reduced fat milk, low fat milk, and fat free milk. Schools can also serve milk without lactose for students who cannot digest regular milk. If a student has a disability that prevents them from drinking milk, schools must provide a replacement drink. A doctor, parent, or legal guardian must write a letter explaining the student's disability and what drink should be used instead. Any milk fat from the milk that schools serve will not count as saturated fat when measuring whether school meals meet nutrition rules. Schools cannot buy or serve milk that comes from companies owned by the Chinese government. The Secretary of Agriculture cannot stop schools from offering any of the types of milk listed in this law.
